signal_title: Consultation on Declaration Functionality for Media Service Providers
risk_rationale: The regulatory signal indicates a new requirement for very large online platforms to implement functionalities allowing media service providers to declare their status. This is a moderate risk as it requires a new feature implementation but does not pose an existential threat or immediate compliance gap.

compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Implement functionalities allowing media service providers to declare their status.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not include a provision for media service providers to declare their status, which is required by the new EU regulatory guidance.

remediations.title: Implement Media Service Provider Declaration Functionality
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a new feature that allows media service providers to declare their status on the platform, in compliance with the European Media Freedom Act.
affected_features: User interface for media service providers
acceptance_criteria: Media service providers can declare their status through a dedicated interface.
The declaration status is visible to content moderation teams to prevent unjustified moderation.
Compliance with the European Media Freedom Act guidelines.

business_impact: The implementation of this functionality may require moderate engineering resources and could impact the content moderation workflow. However, it aligns with regulatory compliance and may enhance relationships with media service providers.
recommended_actions: Initiate a project to scope and design the declaration functionality.
Engage with the European Commission to ensure alignment with the guidelines.
Conduct a pilot test with select media service providers to refine the feature.
